title: lespedeza × macrovirgata
intro: Lespedeza × macrovirgata, also known as Japanese clover or bush clover, is a perennial legume native to East Asia, characterized by its pink or white flowers and trifoliate leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation. Lespedeza × macrovirgata has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, and its consumption is associated with several health benefits, such as improved cardiovascular health and antioxidant activity. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
